<div style="font-family:Courier;>{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 549: "Shattered"}}<div>
{{Blurbackground|file=Original-45a300eb1b17bbe0f38ac22334a40f57.webp}}
<div style="font-family:Courier;>
{{Class Lambda}}
Level 549 is the 550<small>th</small> level of the backrooms.
==<div style="font-family:Courier;>Overview<div><div>==
{{Blurimage|file=Original-45a300eb1b17bbe0f38ac22334a40f57.webp|caption=nothing is left now.}}
Level 549, also known as "Shattered," takes the appearance of a street, seemingly taking place in the mid-2000s. Only around 1,000 feet of the level is covered by the street; the rest is an infinite expanse of grass. The level, as in the name, is distorted and shattered. The level is highly unstable and corrupted. There is a lack of any supplies within the level, and if any wanderer finds one, it is a trap set in place by the entities of this level. 

The level is known for its strange, impossible physics. Pushing or moving objects will cause them to spin uncontrollably and float into the sky. The level also has a complete absence of friction. Walking on the street or sidewalk is equivalent to walking on ice. 

There are 7 homes within the level. The homes are completely empty and are lightly coated with dust. There is a single light in the middle of the roof illuminating the room. Wanderers report burning sensations after touching the light. Seemingly, the light seems to float on its own, as it isn't attached to the ceiling. Rarely, black puddles can be found on the floor. They are presumed to be extremely unstable, and wanderers are highly recommended not to interact with them.

Behind each house is a small entrance to a bunker. The bunker leads to a small blue room with a dark wood floor. A glass pool can be found on the floor. The glass will instantly shred skin upon being touched. A small white door can be found in the left corner. The door leads to another blue room, and this cycle repeats infinitely.

A field of grass surrounds each house. The grass seems to be made out of a plastic-like material, as it is reported to be impossible to tear, rip, or destroy. The grass is cold and damp, presumably covered in rain. Certain patches of the grass are completely frozen over in ice. Oddly, getting near it causes the grass to begin burning. The grass seems to stretch on infinitely; however, it is considered far too dangerous and unstable to explore. 

At the end of the street lies a mansion. The mansion is filled with broken chairs and glass. A faint hum buzz can be heard while in the mansion, similar to [[Level 0]]. Large caution tape prevents wanderers from accessing the 2nd floor and the backyard. Similar black puddles can be found within the mansion as they were in the house.

At the other end of the street, a small wooden door attached to the concrete can be found. The door is locked; however, it can be broken down with enough strength. Opening the door will lead to a dark room with a single light illuminating it. A blue metal chair can be found in the corner. After opening the door, a timer will start, and it will lock permanently in 30 seconds.

Lining the streets are electrical poles. They are made out of hard concrete-like material. Wanderers report a small chance of finding a box attached to the left side. The box is completely empty, but writing can be seen on the inside. Wanderers can use markers to write messages to other wanderers within the level, as you are completely alone within the level.

Large statues can be seen far in the distance from the street. The statues seem to resemble humans; however, they are also able to resemble animals. The statue is made out of a concrete-like material, similar to the sidewalk on the level. The statues seem to be able to move on their own and are presumed to be highly hostile.

A strange event can occur where large cracks open in the middle of the street. Large furniture items will start to begin floating upwards towards the sky. This event lasts around 15 minutes. A loud screeching sound is present the entire time the event takes place. When the event is over, the cracks will slowly start to close up. When the cracks have been fully closed, all of the furniture raining down from the sky will plummet back to the level. Hiding in the houses is safe; however, the mansion will be destroyed by the event. After 3 hours, the mansion will begin to rebuild itself.

==<div style="font-family:Courier;>Entities<div>==

The level's entities are different than normal and have adapted to the level's physics. Entities include Facelings and Skin Stealers. These two entities have adapted to be unable to jump and have developed claws to stop themself without friction. Entities within the level are extremely rare.

==<div style="font-family:Courier;>Colonies and Outposts<div>==

Because of the level's isolating effect, there are no colonies or outposts. 

==<div style="font-family:Courier;>Entrances<div>==
There is no designated entrance to the level; instead, no clipping from any level has a chance to send one here. Levels a wanderer is most likely to noclip from are [[Level 343|343]], [[Level 908|908]], and [[Level 112|112]].

==<div style="font-family:Courier;>Exits<div>==
Within the mansion, there is a small chance of finding a dark blue door. Opening the door will lead the wanderer to [[Level 11]]. Wanderers also have a small chance to noclip when they are trapped in the dark room; however, this is extremely difficult and strenuous.
